_meta.purpose: Stand-in gold for scoring the docs/23 T1 re-runs (ML-E4/E7/E19) against the real-VLM segmentation rebuild produced by `homeinventory.cli build ... --segment-model gpt-4.1-mini`. This is NOT a replacement for the authoritative human-curated evals/fixtures/own-property/hero-gold.json, which was calibrated against a different (lost, never-regenerated) Gemini segmentation run whose exact frame filenames do not exist in this rebuild.
_meta.curator: agent (visual review via docs/18 criteria: establishing framing — walls/floor/ceiling/doorway context, sharp, uncluttered; reject object close-ups, wrong-room bleed, motion blur, partial/odd framing)
_meta.segmentation: homeinventory.cli build capture-walkthrough -o report --segment-model gpt-4.1-mini --backend offline --no-detect --no-pdf (report/work/segments/IMG_5512.json)
_meta.rooms_note: This VLM run split the property into Hallway, Living Room, Kitchen, Bedroom 1, Bathroom, En-suite Shower Room, Loft Room, Stairs and Landing, WC (9 rooms) -- close to but not identical to hero-gold.json's 9 (it merged Loft Bedroom/Loft Shower Room into one 'Loft Room' and separated a 'WC').

Hallway.notes: Wide hallway with wallpaper, rug and doorway context reads best; f000144 is a wrong-room bleed frame (shows the living room TV/sofa through the doorway, not the hallway itself); f010080 is a coat-cupboard close-up.

Living Room.notes: Weak pool: this segment is mostly Kitchen-bleed frames at its start (segment boundary drift) plus a blurred lamp close-up. f009108 (window/table nook) is the only frame that is genuinely establishing for this room. f009441 is actually the hallway again (further boundary bleed, matches the Hallway top pick's tree/rug), and f008676 is a motion-blurred lamp close-up -- both avoid-worthy under docs/18's wrong-room and motion-blur failure modes.

Kitchen.notes: Pool skews heavily toward cabinet/appliance-interior close-ups (open cabinets, fridge, oven). f004068 (sink/splashback/washing machine) and f000495 (counter corner) are the widest, least object-fill shots; f007920 adds window/table context despite clutter. f002214 is a blank, textureless cupboard-interior void; f006102 is a dirty oven-interior close-up -- both fail the 'no object close-up / no empty blur' bar.

Bedroom 1.notes: f021384 (bed, headboard, bedside table) is the one clean establishing shot; f018468 (eaves storage nook) and f017073 (ceiling slope + doorway) are distant seconds. Bottom picks are a blurred blanket/curtain close-up and a sock-drawer close-up. Two frames (IMG_5512_f017973.jpg, IMG_5512_f018036.jpg) were excluded from consideration entirely -- they are mirror/window reflections that incidentally capture the person filming, not usable hero candidates on privacy grounds regardless of composition quality.

Bathroom.notes: f011070 and f010845 both show the toilet+sink+tile suite well; f012132 is a clean shower-enclosure shot. f011340 is a wrong-room bleed frame (shows a bedroom/office with a desk chair, not the bathroom); f011871 is a dark, motion-blurred doorway pass.

En-suite Shower Room.notes: Weak pool dominated by shower-head/fixture close-ups. f022212 (tiled shower corner with fixture) is the best available; f022149 and f023715 are ceiling/floor/door-edge shots that at least show more than one surface. f023319 (dark, blurred cabinet interior) and f023526 (motion-blurred countertop) are the clearest blur violations.

Loft Room.notes: f014922 is a strong wide establishing shot (sofa/beanbag, lamp, full room depth). f016038 (skylight nook + radiator) and f015246 (framed shirt + whiteboard wall) are reasonable seconds. f016263 is a dark, blurred skylight close-up; f016695 is a severely motion-blurred door/landing transition.

Stairs and Landing.notes: Only 4 frames total, all reasonable. f014211 and f014391 are clean, symmetric full-flight staircase views; f014679 is the landing corridor (no stairs visible, but represents the 'Landing' half of the room name). f014076 is the weakest -- an off-angle, partially cropped view -- so it doubles as the bottom pick alongside the landing shot's lack of staircase content.

WC.notes: Caveat: none of this room's 4 frames actually show a WC/toilet fixture -- this looks like a segmentation-boundary artifact (the pool is an understairs storage nook plus stairs/landing bleed, not a genuine WC). f013374 (storage nook) is the least-bad pick; f013005/f013014 are near-duplicate blurred doorway passes and f013905 is a handrail close-up that is really Stairs-and-Landing content. Treat this room's gold as low-confidence given the underlying pool has no on-topic frame at all.
